privacy.title: Privacy Notice
description: This website is designed to collect as little information as possible. The data it
collect is used to identify which chess lines and topics are frequently viewed, so that new or updated content
can focus on those areas. It will
be shared with any third parties.
The site does not use advertisements, third-party analytics, or cookies.
When the page is loaded, it creates a random session ID. This is replaced with a new random ID each time
the page is reloaded, and is not used to recognise returning visitors.
During a session, the website may record:

value: the page was loaded, a move was made or undone, or a glossary topic was opened
, and any moves stored in the
Some additional non-identifying metadata (specifically, the HTTP request method and the API route)

description: This information is used only to understand how visitors interact with the site's content, with the goal of
improving it.
Like pretty much every website on the internet, the server necessarily processes technical information
(such as your IP address) in order to deliver web pages to your browser or to collect the data listed above.
This technical information is only used directly for operating the website, and is
intentionally stored. The server is specifically configured not to generate its standard access logs for
visits to the site.
The site is hosted by a third-party provider, which also necessarily processes technical information
required to deliver the website. Unfortunately, I can't control what data they store.
As is stated in the
section, this project is open source. If you're interested in exactly how the site collects data,
please feel free to inspect the code.

welcome.title: Welcome to Cogit
description: The goal of this project is to function as a comprehensive resource for chess improvement. Its contents
are written and edited to be concise, without omitting important details, and approachable
even to beginner players.
It is assumed that the reader knows the rules of the game, but nothing beyond that is required.
Individual moves are referenced using
, which is standard practice in the chess world.
For each move that is played on the interactive chess board, relevant information explaining its strengths
and drawbacks will be retrieved and shown in the
box located directly above this article.
All commentary is written manually move-by-move, to ensure that it is of
high quality.

value: Due to the exponential nature of chess, it is not possible to exhaustively cover every possible move sequence
in this fashion; instead, the available commentary is focused primarily in common chess openings, notable
chess games, and custom positions that can be reached fairly quickly from those points.

description: The
segment just underneath the chess board displays possible moves from the current board position
which have corresponding information (or for which a subsequent move has corresponding information)
The segment in which
is displayed is the
. In order to keep move commentary lean, general information is arranged into glossary topics, which can be
accessed via the margin to the left.
If a topic is relevant or necessary to understand
the currently displayed commentary, a button which opens that topic will be present in the commentary section.
This layout is designed to make it easy to get started - play moves on the chess board, read through the
associated commentary, and click on embedded buttons to view more in-depth explanations
for any unfamiliar concepts.
